Home Archive

Filament wye

Posted on March 11, 2026
Tags: c, raylib

instructions

interactive web app

Drag the circles and ticks. Watch the wye fall into place, but sometimes it gets lost so reload the page or press the space bar to reset. Sliders modify parameters to be explained.

source

background

Continuing from spline decimation 2026-02-04, I try to answer: is the usual symmetrical wye connector like the one here optimal? Maybe asymmetry is better, since the filaments have different properties, and the one more likely to clog can go on the straight path.

The capstan equation is for flexible materials. There’s an optimal path that’s circular arcs or maybe a logarithmic spiral, and the examples give an intuition we don’t need to develop. For that model, the smoothy y has outputs parallel but this adds extra turns unless the input filament reels are 1cm wide. But filament stiffness might change things, since bending might oppose tension. So the objective is the final filament tension (T(1)) solved using:

dT/dt = μv(Tκ + EIκ″)

T(0) = 0

v = hypot(dxdt, dydt)

where κ is the curvature, and we have some complications because the x(t) y(t) splines are parameterized by an arbitrary parameter not arc length and κ″ should be a derivative with respect to arc length.

Then gsl_multimin_fminimizer_nmsimplex2 picks the points.

Site proudly generated by Hakyll
